classification_rationale: This is the AFI governance/DAO plane, NOT one of the five canonical signal-data planes. README.md:13 "AFI Governance is the agentic decision-making layer of the AFI Protocol." It defines a governance "Universal Proposal Signal" schema plus Snapshot/Safe/Zodiac-Reality execution. Under the North Star, registries/governance "influence SELECTION & ALLOCATION only -- MUST NOT override deterministic scoring or protocol finality," so this repo is outside the normative scoring/commitment/evidence surface. It is classified REFERENCE_IMPL rather than NORMATIVE because (a) it picks a SPECIFIC governance stack (Snapshot off-chain + Safe multisig + Zodiac Reality module) as the execution path, and (b) nearly all logic is stubs/placeholders (validator/proposal_scorer.ts returns Math.random(); cli/submit-proposal.ts is a console.log; agents/proposal_executor_agent.ts and SAFE REALITY/safe.ts are empty bodies). It is not DOCS because it carries a (partially) machine-readable proposal schema + a working zod validation harness. It is not STALE overall (HEAD commit 2026-01-24), though it contains stale sub-artifacts (docs/governance_links.md, sprint_archive).
primary_purpose: Defines the AFI governance/DAO layer: a standardized "Universal Proposal Signal" schema for human/agent governance proposals, agent-validator scoring stubs, a deferred-staking activation threshold, and a hybrid off-chain (Snapshot) -> on-chain (Safe + Zodiac Reality) proposal execution model.
protocol_touchpoints: registries/reputation (validator registry, deferred staking, agent registry schema)
replay/determinism (mentioned aspirationally only: 'Replayable DAG of proposal analysis' - not implemented)
mint/emissions (referenced only via MIN_SUPPLY_FOR_STAKING staking-activation gate, no mint logic)
normative_artifacts: /home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-governance/schemas/UniversalProposalSignal.schema.json (governance proposal schema - NOTE: not valid JSON, contains JS comments+prose)
/home/user/AFI-Protocol/afi-governance/schemas/SignalChallengeProposal.schema.json (valid draft-07 schema for Snapshot signal-challenge proposals)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: Safe Reality is the integration of: Snapshot ... Zodiac Reality Module ... Safe Multisig — for secure execution of governance proposals.
Hybrid Execution (Snapshot + Safe + Reality) ... Snapshot for gasless off-chain signaling - Safe for treasury control ... Zodiac Reality module for enforcement
Schema for AFI signal challenge proposals submitted to Snapshot governance
All governance actions must comply with this schema to be valid under the AFI Protocol.
🚀 FastAPI Registry Server Endpoints / GET /agents / POST /register

contradictions.title: Repo described as 'on-chain governance contracts' but contains zero contracts
contradictions.detail: AGENTS.md and package.json present this as a Solidity/contracts repo, but there are no .sol files anywhere (git ls-files '*.sol' is empty) and the AGENTS.md-claimed directories (contracts/, src/, proposals/, pulse/) do not exist. All on-chain execution is off-chain stubs (Snapshot/Safe SDK with empty function bodies). Misleads architectural decisions about where on-chain governance enforcement lives.
contradictions.evidence: AGENTS.md:3 'afi-governance contains on-chain governance contracts and Epoch Pulse coordination logic'; AGENTS.md:49-52 lists contracts/ src/ proposals/ pulse/ — all MISSING; package.json:4 'AFI governance contracts and Epoch Pulse coordination'

contradictions.title: 'Universal Proposal Signal' reuses canonical 'signal' vocabulary for a non-canonical concept
contradictions.detail: The repo treats governance proposals as 'signals' (signal_type: governance_proposal) and calls its schema the canonical 'Universal Proposal Signal,' colliding with the protocol-canonical USS v1.1 micro-signal (afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1/). These are different objects; conflating them risks an external integrator treating governance proposals as scoreable evidence signals or vice versa. SignalChallengeProposal further references real UWR fields (baseScore/decayScore/analystId/market) bridging governance into the scoring plane.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/UniversalProposalSignal.schema.json:5 '"signal_type": "governance_proposal"'; README.md:38-40 'Universal Proposal Signal ... follow a standardized schema'; SignalChallengeProposal.schema.json:62-91 baseScore/decayScore/analystId 'Original UWR score'

contradictions.title: Canonical schema file is not valid JSON (un-parseable as a schema)
contradictions.detail: schemas/UniversalProposalSignal.schema.json is declared the 'canonical schema' but is not machine-readable JSON: it begins with // comments, embeds prose paragraphs, numbered scoring-flow steps, and multiple unrelated JSON blocks (agent registry, FastAPI endpoints). An external validator cannot load it. The actual enforced schema is a hand-written zod object in the test harness, which can silently drift from the doc.
contradictions.evidence: schemas/UniversalProposalSignal.schema.json:1-2 '// Mirrors specs/... // Used internally'; specs/universal_proposal_schema.json:1-3 same; vs afi_proposal_schema_test/proposal_schema.test.ts:5-26 zod schema is the real validator

contradictions.title: Governance/staking gated on mint supply implies tight coupling to commitment plane that is not wired
contradictions.detail: Staking and vote-weighted governance are deferred until 1e9 AFI minted (MIN_SUPPLY_FOR_STAKING), implying a dependency on afi-token/afi-mint emissions state, but there is no code reading on-chain supply — it is a documented intent only. Governance allocation power therefore has an undefined link to the commitment plane.
contradictions.evidence: config/config_staking.ts:4 'export const MIN_SUPPLY_FOR_STAKING = 1_000_000_000;'; README.md:26-31 'activate automatically after 1,000,000,000 AFI tokens have been minted'

contradictions.title: docs/governance_links.md points to files that do not exist at HEAD (stale pinned refs)
contradictions.detail: governance_links.md links 'Immutable References' at commit 06ab9c3 (paper-v1): constitution.md, schemas/governance_schema.ts, roles/role_definitions.md, tokenomics/distribution_model.ts, tokenomics/staking_rights.md, org/board_structure.md — none of these paths exist in the current tree (roles/ tokenomics/ org/ all MISSING). Stale provenance doc.
contradictions.evidence: docs/governance_links.md:8-31 links to docs/constitution.md, tokenomics/distribution_model.ts, org/board_structure.md; filesystem check: roles/ tokenomics/ org/ MISSING

upstream: afi-config (AGENTS.md:7,108 cites afi-config/codex/governance/droids/AFI_DROID_CHARTER.v0.1.md as global authority; AGENTS.md:81 'validated against afi-config schemas')
afi-token / afi-mint (staking gated on AFI minted supply, MIN_SUPPLY_FOR_STAKING)
Snapshot.org (off-chain voting, SignalChallengeProposal schema + executor agent)
Safe / Zodiac Reality module (on-chain execution layer, external SDKs)
downstream: afi-token (AGENTS.md:54 'Consumed by: afi-token, afi-reactor, on-chain governance processes')

notable_findings.title: Governance proposal schema duplicated across two files with no single source of truth
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/UniversalProposalSignal.schema.json:1 '// Mirrors specs/universal_proposal_schema.json for Zenodo lineage'; specs/universal_proposal_schema.json:1-30 identical body. Both un-parseable as JSON.
notable_findings.recommendation: Pick one canonical machine-readable JSON Schema (draft-07 like SignalChallengeProposal), generate the zod validator from it, and delete the prose-laden duplicate to prevent drift.

notable_findings.title: All governance execution logic is stubs / placeholders
notable_findings.evidence: validator/proposal_scorer.ts:2-5 'return Math.random();'; validator/proposal_validator.ts:2-4 returns truthiness only; cli/submit-proposal.ts:2 console.log placeholder; agents/proposal_executor_agent.ts:3-8 empty body; SAFE REALITY/safe.ts:3-6 empty body.
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat repo as scaffolding/reference; do not cite its scoring as protocol logic. Real proposal scoring (novelty/risk/alignment) is undefined.

notable_findings.title: Validator registry uses hardcoded trust scores (no on-chain/registry binding)
notable_findings.recommendation: If governance selection is to honor REGISTRIES_AND_REPUTATION.v0.1.md, bind validator trust to the canonical registry rather than a hardcoded array; clarify this is placeholder.

notable_findings.title: SignalChallengeProposal bridges governance into the scoring/UWR plane via Snapshot
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/SignalChallengeProposal.schema.json:62-91 signalMetadata.baseScore 'Original UWR score', decayScore 'Time-decayed UWR score', analystId, market; :5 'submitted to Snapshot governance'
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirm challenge-via-vote does NOT override deterministic scoring/finality (North Star rule); a Snapshot vote should at most flag/reallocate, never mutate canonical UWR. Verify against VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC.v0.1.md.

notable_findings.title: 'access_tssd' permission referenced in agent registry schema but no TSSD/vault code here
notable_findings.evidence: schemas/UniversalProposalSignal.schema.json:79 '"permissions": ["score_signals", "submit_proposals", "access_tssd"]'; specs/universal_proposal_schema.json:80 same. No mongodb/vault/VaultedSignalRecord implementation in repo.
notable_findings.recommendation: Cross-reference the canonical TSSD spec; ensure governance-agent TSSD access is gated by the evidence-plane public-vs-proprietary surface rules, not granted blanket via a governance schema.

notable_findings.title: Replayability/determinism mentioned only aspirationally for governance
notable_findings.evidence: docs/sprint_archive/README_sprint.md:65 'Replayable DAG of proposal analysis' (under Future Enhancements); schemas/README.md:19 'Proposal Replay & Insight Review'. No replay artifacts or determinism rules implemented.
notable_findings.recommendation: No replay-critical artifacts here. Governance proposal scoring is non-deterministic by design (Math.random stub); keep it outside the deterministic scoring-DAG replay surface.

notable_findings.title: FastAPI registry server design in schema doc has no implementation (Python absent; repo is TS-only)
notable_findings.evidence: specs/universal_proposal_schema.json:92-106 'FastAPI Registry Server Endpoints / GET /agents / POST /register / PATCH /agents/{id}/compliance'; repo file types: only .ts/.json/.md (no .py).
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag as design/aspiration; remove or relocate to a design doc so the schema file is purely a schema.

notable_findings.title: Epoch Pulse coordination claimed as a core purpose but absent from code
notable_findings.evidence: package.json:4 'AFI governance contracts and Epoch Pulse coordination'; AGENTS.md:46 'Define ... Epoch Pulse coordination'; AGENTS.md:82 'Follow design in pulse/design.md' — pulse/ directory MISSING.
notable_findings.recommendation: Either remove Epoch Pulse claims or add the pulse/ design; current state misrepresents repo scope.

notable_findings.title: No USS v1.1 / CPJ ingest, no mint/receipt contracts, no Mongo/BigQuery/Postgres present
notable_findings.evidence: grep for usignal|USS|cpj => 0 hits; mintForSignal|coordinateMint => 0 hits; AFIMintCoordinator|AFISignalReceipt|EmissionsMinted => 0 hits; mongodb|BigQuery|kafka|postgres|timescale|influx => 0 hits.
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirms this repo touches none of the canonical ingest/commitment/evidence/analytics stores; classify firmly as the governance plane only.
